parentQuestion: Severe stabbing facial pain triggered by light touch

scaffolds.q: The trigeminal nerve (CN V) carries sensation from the face.
scaffolds.explain: Touch, pain, temperature on your face travel through CN V.
Neuro = nerve, algia = pain. Trigeminal neuralgia is pain from CN V.

scaffolds.q: Some nerve conditions cause extreme pain from normal touch.
scaffolds.explain: This is called allodynia - pain from something that should not hurt.

scaffolds.q: Lancinating pain means sharp, shooting, stabbing pain.
scaffolds.explain: Like an electric shock or knife stab. Very intense but brief.

scaffolds.q: Trigeminal neuralgia causes brief but severe facial pain attacks.
scaffolds.explain: The pain lasts seconds but is excruciating. Triggered by touch, chewing, wind.

scaffolds.q: CN V has three branches covering forehead, cheek, and jaw.
scaffolds.explain: Trigeminal neuralgia usually affects one branch, often the cheek area.

scaffolds.q: A normal neurological exam with only pain suggests sensory nerve problem.
scaffolds.explain: No weakness, no numbness, just severe pain attacks = nerve hypersensitivity.

scaffolds.q: Trigeminal neuralgia is also called tic douloureux (painful tic).
scaffolds.explain: The intense pain can cause facial twitching or wincing.

scaffolds.q: Triggers for trigeminal neuralgia include touch, chewing, cold wind.
scaffolds.explain: Light stimuli trigger severe pain - patients avoid these triggers.
